📈 Detailed Waveform Analysis

Click tabs below to view detailed analysis of converter operation in different conditions

Steady-State Switching Waveforms

Shows switch node voltage, inductor current, and output voltage during continuous conduction mode operation. PWM frequency: 100kHz.

Startup Transient Response

Output voltage ramps from 0V to 5V during converter startup. Shows control loop response and inductor current buildup.

Load Step Response

Dynamic response to step change in load current. Demonstrates control loop stability and transient undershoot/recovery characteristics.

Efficiency Curve Analysis

Converter efficiency versus output power. Peak efficiency of 92.8% at rated load (1A @ 5V).

Loss Breakdown Analysis

Power loss distribution among circuit components. Diode conduction loss dominates at 356.2mW.

Control Loop Bode Plot

Frequency response of the error amplifier and control loop. Demonstrates stability margins and bandwidth characteristics.

🔧 Design Methodology

Six-step systematic approach to synchronous buck converter design

1
Specifications Definition
Input: 24V, Output: 5V, Power: 5W, Load: 1A, Switching Frequency: 100kHz
2
Duty Cycle Calculation
D = Vout/Vin = 5/24 = 20.8%. Controls switch on-time and power transfer ratio.
3
Inductor Sizing
L = 47.0µH selected for 0.94A peak-peak ripple (~102.4% of average current)
4
Output Capacitor Selection
C = 470.0µF optimized for 29.3mV ripple and transient response
5
Control Loop Compensation
PI compensator with 10kΩ resistor and 10nF capacitor for stable regulation
6
SPICE Simulation & Validation
Transient analysis confirms 4.62V output with 92.8% efficiency
📋 Component Parameters (BOM)

Bill of Materials and design component values

Component Value/Type Purpose
Input Capacitor (Cin) 100µF Input filtering, charge storage
High-Side MOSFET (S1) Synchronous Switch Power stage, RON=25mΩ
Low-Side Diode (D1) Schottky, 40V Rating Freewheeling, IS=1µA, RS=10mΩ
Output Inductor (L1) 47.0µH Current smoothing, DCR=50mΩ
Output Capacitor (Cout) 470.0µF Voltage ripple filtering, ESR=15mΩ
Load Resistor 5.0Ω Load emulation, 1A @ 5V
Comp Resistor 10kΩ PI compensator, error feedback path
Comp Capacitor 10nF Integrator, loop stabilization
⚡ Power Loss Distribution

Detailed breakdown of 473.5mW total power dissipation at rated load

Loss Source Power (mW) Percentage
MOSFET Conduction Loss 5.2 1.1%
Diode Conduction Loss 356.2 75.2%
Inductor DCR Loss 50.0 10.6%
Switching Loss 42.0 8.9%
Gate Drive Loss 20.0 4.2%
Total Dissipated Power 473.5 100.0%
💻 SPICE Netlist

Complete circuit definition for NGSPICE simulation

* Buck DC-DC Converter - Synchronous Topology * Input: 24V DC, Output: 5V DC @ 1A * Switching Frequency: 100kHz * Designed for CCM operation * * === Power Stage === V_in vin 0 DC 24 * Input filter capacitor C_in vin 0 100u IC=24 * High-side MOSFET (behavioral switch) S1 vin sw ctrl_h 0 SMOD .model SMOD SW(RON=25m ROFF=1MEG VT=0.5) * Low-side Schottky diode D1 0 sw SCHOTTKY .model SCHOTTKY D(IS=1e-5 N=1.05 RS=0.01 BV=40 CJO=100p) * Output inductor with DCR L1 sw lx 47u IC=0 R_dcr lx vout 50m * Output filter capacitor with ESR R_esr vout vout_c 15m C_out vout_c 0 470u IC=0 * Load R_load vout 0 5 * === PWM Control === * 100kHz sawtooth V_ramp ramp 0 PULSE(0 1 0 {1/100k} 1n 1n {1/100k}) * Error amplifier (PI compensator) E_err err 0 VOL='5.0 - V(vout)' R_comp err comp 10k C_comp comp 0 10n * PWM comparator B_pwm ctrl_h 0 V = V(comp) > V(ramp) ? 1 : 0 * === Analysis === .tran 50n 5m UIC .control run plot v(sw) title 'Switch Node Voltage' plot i(L1) title 'Inductor Current' plot v(vout) title 'Output Voltage' meas tran vout_avg AVG v(vout) from=4m to=5m meas tran vout_ripple PP v(vout) from=4m to=5m meas tran il_avg AVG i(L1) from=4m to=5m meas tran il_ripple PP i(L1) from=4m to=5m .endc .end
